Description: Discovered a potential authorization token provided in a curl command header, which could compromise the curl accessed resource.
Author: mattg-stack
Message:
feat(scripts): interactive system-health-check.sh

Bash walk-through of every major subsystem with PASS/WARN/FAIL and
next-step hints. Used for interactive verification and as a fallback
when Grafana isn't available. Sections:

- infra (Docker, Postgres, worker, disk)
- db (tables, activity, queue, embeddings)
- models (Ollama, writer model, SDXL, voice bot)
- observability (Grafana, Prometheus, Alertmanager)
- alerts (Discord webhook, Telegram decrypt, policy)
- pipeline (job count, approval rate, stuck tasks)
- publish (static export, site, posts, podcasts, videos)
- secrets (encrypted app_settings decrypt correctly)

Usage:
  bash scripts/system-health-check.sh            # interactive
  bash scripts/system-health-check.sh --all      # no prompts
  bash scripts/system-health-check.sh --only=alerts,publish

Exit codes: 0=all pass, 1=any warn, 2=any fail. Grafana-dashboard
companion (poindexter-system-health) lands in the next commit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Description: Detected a Generic API Key, potentially exposing access to various services and sensitive operations.
Author: mattg-stack
Message:
feat(observability): lifespan re-init + Grafana dashboards + scrape targets

Follow-up to the LGTM+P + Kuma + GlitchTip wire (79bbe241). Three fixes
+ four dashboards on top of the initial scaffold.

**Lifespan re-init pattern.** setup_sentry / setup_telemetry /
setup_pyroscope all run at module-level import, but site_config.load()
runs inside the lifespan handler — so the first pass saw empty DB
values for sentry_dsn / enable_tracing / enable_pyroscope. Re-invoking
the three setups inside the lifespan, after site_config.load(), is the
first point where the DB values are actually populated. Each setup is
idempotent + guarded.

**sentry_integration.py hardening.**
  • SQLAlchemy integration import raised sentry_sdk.DidNotEnable (not
    ImportError) because our stack uses asyncpg/psycopg directly. Now
    caught as `except Exception`, with the integration conditionally
    added only when non-None.
  • Removed the premature `_initialized = True` latch when the DSN is
    empty. The latch meant the lifespan re-invocation would short-circuit
    to "already initialized" and the real DSN would never take effect.

**prometheus.yml — three new scrape jobs.**
  • uptime-kuma — password-only basic auth with the API key stored in
    bootstrap.toml as `uptime_kuma_api_key`. Surfaces per-monitor
    response_time + status to Grafana.
  • tempo — internal metrics (ingest rate, block uploads, spanmetrics).
  • pyroscope — self-metrics for ingestion rate + active profiles.

**docker-compose.local.yml healthcheck fixes.**
  • glitchtip-web: image ships neither wget nor curl; switched to
    Python urllib probing /api/0/ (public, no auth required).
  • pyroscope: distroless image has no shell, no probe tooling. Set
    healthcheck.disable = true — the honest state. Grafana + worker
    both exercise the service end-to-end on every scrape/ingest, so
    failure still surfaces immediately via Prometheus.

**Four Grafana dashboards — auto-provisioned.**
  • obs-stack-overview — scrape-health stat grid, Tempo+Pyroscope+Kuma
    rollups, GlitchTip link panel.
  • obs-tempo — spanmetrics RED panels (rate / p95 latency / error%),
    service graph, TraceQL error search.
  • obs-pyroscope — CPU flamegraph + per-service ingestion timeseries.
  • obs-uptime — monitor status stat grid, response time timeseries,
    30m rolling uptime %, TLS expiry panel.

All four land in infrastructure/grafana/dashboards/ and are picked up
by the existing provisioning config (path: /etc/grafana/dashboards,
updateIntervalSeconds: 30). Verified live via /api/search — all four
UIDs (obs-stack-overview, obs-tempo, obs-pyroscope, obs-uptime) are
loaded by Grafana.

Description: Detected a Generic API Key, potentially exposing access to various services and sensitive operations.
Author: mattg-stack
Message:
hardening: pipeline autonomy, backup automation, QA improvements, bootstrap fixes

Pipeline:
- Idle worker gate: removed 'approved' from blocking statuses, moved non-GPU tasks before gate
- Topic discovery: filter academic titles, ALLCAPS rejection, intra-batch dedup, min-length check
- Thinking model support: salvage answers from thinking traces, 6x token multiplier for content gen
- Truncation detection: content validator flags articles ending mid-sentence as critical
- Title generation: bumped max_tokens to 4000 for thinking model headroom
- QA critic token budget: 1500→8000 for thinking models, 300→1500 standard
- Dev.to tag truncation: enforce 30-char max per tag

Infrastructure:
- Database backups: pg_dump + R2 upload + JSON export, 12h schedule, 14-day retention
- postgresql-client added to worker Dockerfile
- Composite indexes: content_tasks(status, created_at), posts(status, published_at)
- Migration 0064 function name fix (run→run_migration)
- Bootstrap: fixed psql port 5433→15432, real DB connectivity check, visible hardware detection
- Bootstrap: QA token seeds match code defaults (8000/1500)
- IndexNow: real key in publish service instead of placeholder

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Author: mattg-stack
Message:
fix: HN launch hardening — SEO, security, performance

SEO (critical):
- Fix canonical URLs and og:url from glad-labs.com to www.gladlabs.io on all blog posts
- Fix missing /posts/ path in canonical URLs
- Fix keywords meta tag character-split bug (was "m,o,d,e,l" instead of "model")
- Add og:image, og:url, canonical to homepage
- Fix JSON-LD structured data wrong domain
- Fix email/links in 404 and error pages

Security:
- Rate limit GDPR data-requests endpoint (5/min)
- Disable API docs (/api/docs, /api/redoc) in production
- Remove X-Powered-By: Next.js header

Performance:
- Increase homepage ISR revalidation from 5min to 15min for traffic spikes
- Parallelize main post + related posts fetches (eliminate waterfall)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Message:
refactor: split task_routes.py into 4 sub-routers (#1201)

2,800-line file split into domain sub-routers:
- task_routes.py (859) — CRUD, list, metrics, create + type registry
- task_status_routes.py (667) — status transitions, history, updates
- task_publishing_routes.py (1,180) — approve, publish, reject, image gen

All 19 routes registered via include_router, 48 task_routes tests pass.

Closes #1201

Author: mattg-stack
Message:
fix: resolve all P0 security issues (#276, #285, #292, #296, #305, #313, #382, #264, #395)

Backend auth fixes:
- Gate get_current_user_optional dev bypass on DEVELOPMENT_MODE=true (#276)
- Gate get_user_id() "test-user-123" fallback on DEVELOPMENT_MODE=true (#285)
- Gate DISABLE_AUTH_FOR_DEV bypass on ENVIRONMENT != production (#292)
- Add router-level auth to entire /api/workflows router (#296)
- Add auth to task metrics endpoints (#382)
- Replace mid-request DatabaseService() with pool injection in cms_routes (#264)
- Fix Poetry lockfile: pin pytest-benchmark to ^4.0 (#395)

Frontend XSS fixes:
- PostEditor.jsx: wrap dangerouslySetInnerHTML with DOMPurify.sanitize() (#305)
- posts/[slug]/page.tsx: sanitize post.content with sanitize-html before render (#313)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Developed unit tests for TaskTable component focusing on rendering, selection, pagination, and action buttons.
- Implemented unit tests for useTaskData hook to validate pagination, sorting, auto-refresh, error handling, and loading states.
- Introduced a centralized MUI styles file to maintain consistent theming across the application, including color palettes, spacing, and reusable style objects.

- Created test_constraint_utils.py for unit testing constraint handling utilities, covering extraction, validation, and compliance reporting.
- Developed ConstraintComplianceDisplay component to visually represent constraint compliance metrics in the oversight-hub frontend.

Message:
Add system status check and various tests for Strapi and PostgreSQL publisher

- Implemented `system_status.py` to check the connectivity of key services.
- Created `test_graphql.py` for testing GraphQL mutations in Strapi.
- Added `test_graphql_get.py` to test GraphQL GET requests.
- Developed `test_strapi_post.py` to create posts directly in Strapi.
- Introduced `test_strapi_publisher.py` to test the new PostgreSQL-based StrapiPublisher.
- Implemented `test_tasks.py` to retrieve and display task information from the API.
- Added `test_publisher_output.txt` for logging publisher outputs.

Message:
Add diagnostic and testing scripts for task pipeline

- Created `test_imports.py` to verify imports for TaskExecutor and DatabaseService.
- Added `test_api_to_strapi.ps1` for testing API to Strapi pipeline with detailed steps.
- Introduced `test_pipeline_quick.ps1` for a quick start guide to test the API to Strapi pipeline.

Author: mattg-stack
Message:
Add MetricsDisplay and TaskCreationModal components with functionality for displaying metrics and creating blog post tasks

- Implement MetricsDisplay component to show aggregated task execution metrics with auto-refresh feature.
- Create MetricCard subcomponent for reusable metric display.
- Integrate Zustand for state management of metrics.
- Implement TaskCreationModal for creating new blog post tasks with real-time progress updates.
- Include form validation and error handling in TaskCreationModal.
- Add polling for task status and display progress in a stepper format.

Author: mattyglads
Message:
feat: initialize Strapi backend with essential configurations and post API

- Added package.json with dependencies and scripts for Strapi application.
- Created robots.txt to manage search engine indexing.
- Added .gitkeep files to maintain directory structure for uploads and API.
- Implemented admin app configuration and TypeScript settings.
- Set up Vite configuration for the admin panel.
- Defined post content type schema with attributes for Title, Slug, BodyContent, PostStatus, Keywords, MetaDescription, FeaturedImage, ImageAltText, and Author.
- Created core controller, router, and service for post management.
- Established TypeScript definitions for generated content types and components.
- Configured TypeScript settings for the backend application.
